title.raw: 《南越列傳》 Treatise on the Nanyue

sentences.zh: 南越王尉佗者，真定人也，姓趙氏。

translations.literal: The King of Nanyue, Wei Tuo, was a man of Zhending, surnamed Zhao.
translations.idiomatic: The King of Nanyue, Wei Tuo, was a native of Zhending. His surname was Zhao.

sentences.zh: 秦時已并天下，略定楊越，置桂林、南海、象郡，以謫徙民，與越雜處十三歲。

translations.literal: When Qin had already unified all under heaven, it conquered and pacified Yangyue, established the commanderies of Guilin, Nanhai, and Xiang, and relocated exiled settlers there, who lived intermixed with the Yue for thirteen years.
translations.idiomatic: After Qin unified the realm, it conquered the Yangyue region and established the commanderies of Guilin, Nanhai, and Xiang. Exiled settlers were sent to live among the Yue people, and the two groups dwelt together for thirteen years.

sentences.zh: 佗，秦時用為南海龍川令。

translations.literal: Tuo was employed by Qin as the Magistrate of Longchuan in Nanhai.
translations.idiomatic: During the Qin dynasty, Tuo served as the Magistrate of Longchuan in the Nanhai commandery.

sentences.zh: 至二世時，南海尉任囂病且死，召龍川令趙佗語曰：「聞陳勝等作亂，秦為無道，天下苦之，項羽、劉季、陳勝、吳廣等州郡各共興軍聚眾，虎爭天下，中國擾亂，未知所安，豪傑畔秦相立。

translations.literal: When the time of the Second Emperor came, the Commandant of Nanhai, Ren Xiao, fell ill and was about to die. He summoned the Magistrate of Longchuan, Zhao Tuo, and said: "I have heard that Chen Sheng and others have raised rebellion, that Qin practices lawlessness and all under heaven suffer from it. Xiang Yu, Liu Ji, Chen Sheng, Wu Guang, and others in their commanderies and districts have each raised armies and gathered followers, contending like tigers for the realm. The Central States are in turmoil, and no one knows where safety lies. Heroes have rebelled against Qin and established themselves as rulers."
translations.idiomatic: When the Second Emperor's reign began, the Commandant of Nanhai, Ren Xiao, fell gravely ill and lay near death. He summoned Zhao Tuo, the Magistrate of Longchuan, and told him: "I have heard that Chen Sheng and the others have risen in revolt. Qin has ruled without the Way, and the whole realm suffers for it. Xiang Yu, Liu Ji, Chen Sheng, Wu Guang, and men across the commanderies and districts have all raised armies and gathered followers, fighting over the empire like tigers. The Central Plains are thrown into chaos, and no one knows where peace will come from. Great men everywhere have turned against Qin and set themselves up as rival lords."

sentences.zh: 南海僻遠，吾恐盜兵侵地至此，吾欲興兵絕新道，自備，待諸侯變，會病甚。

translations.literal: Nanhai is remote and distant. I fear that rebel soldiers will invade and reach this place. I wished to raise troops and cut off the new roads, to prepare our own defenses, and to await changes among the feudal lords, but then I fell gravely ill.
translations.idiomatic: "Nanhai is remote and far from the center. I fear that rebel forces may push their attacks all the way here. I had intended to raise troops and seal off the mountain passes, fortify our defenses, and wait to see how the situation among the feudal lords develops—but now I have fallen gravely ill."

sentences.zh: 且番禺負山險，阻南海，東西數千里，頗有中國人相輔，此亦一州之主也，可以立國。

translations.literal: Moreover, Panyu has its back to steep mountains, is bordered by the Southern Sea, stretches thousands of li from east to west, and has quite a number of people from the Central States to assist. This too can be the domain of a provincial lord, and a kingdom can be established here.
translations.idiomatic: "Furthermore, Panyu is backed by rugged mountains and fronted by the Southern Sea, with territory stretching thousands of li from east to west. A fair number of people from the Central Plains are here to lend their support. This land can certainly sustain a regional lord, and a kingdom may well be founded here."

sentences.zh: 郡中長吏無足與言者，故召公告之。」

translations.literal: Among the senior officials in the commandery, there is none worthy of discussing this with, so I have summoned you to inform you of this.
translations.idiomatic: "Among the senior officials of the commandery, none are fit to discuss such matters. That is why I summoned you to tell you all this."

sentences.zh: 即被佗書，行南海尉事。

translations.literal: He then conferred upon Tuo a written mandate, appointing him to carry out the duties of the Commandant of Nanhai.
translations.idiomatic: He then issued Tuo a formal commission, entrusting him with the duties of the Commandant of Nanhai.

sentences.zh: 囂死，佗即移檄告橫浦、陽山、湟谿關曰：「盜兵且至，急絕道聚兵自守！」

translations.literal: When Xiao died, Tuo immediately sent dispatches to the passes at Hengpu, Yangshan, and Huangxi, saying: "Rebel soldiers are approaching! Urgently cut off the roads, gather troops, and defend yourselves!"
translations.idiomatic: After Ren Xiao died, Tuo immediately dispatched orders to the passes at Hengpu, Yangshan, and Huangxi: "Rebel forces are on their way! Cut off the roads at once, muster your troops, and hold your positions!"

sentences.zh: 因稍以法誅秦所置長吏，以其黨為假守。

translations.literal: He then gradually used legal pretexts to execute the senior officials that Qin had appointed, and replaced them with his own followers as acting administrators.
translations.idiomatic: He then found legal pretexts to gradually eliminate the senior officials Qin had installed, replacing them with his own associates as acting administrators.

sentences.zh: 秦已破滅，佗即擊并桂林、象郡，自立為南越武王。

translations.literal: After Qin was destroyed, Tuo attacked and annexed the commanderies of Guilin and Xiang, and proclaimed himself the Martial King of Nanyue.
translations.idiomatic: Once Qin had fallen, Tuo attacked and absorbed the commanderies of Guilin and Xiang, then proclaimed himself the Martial King of Nanyue.

sentences.zh: 高帝已定天下，為中國勞苦，故釋佗弗誅。

translations.literal: After Emperor Gaozu had settled all under heaven, because the people of the Central States were weary and suffering, he pardoned Tuo and did not punish him.
translations.idiomatic: After Emperor Gaozu had pacified the realm, the people of the Central Plains were exhausted from years of war, so he pardoned Tuo and refrained from punishing him.

sentences.zh: 漢十一年，遣陸賈因立佗為南越王，與剖符通使，和集百越，毋為南邊患害，與長沙接境。

translations.literal: In the eleventh year of Han, he dispatched Lu Jia to formally install Tuo as King of Nanyue, exchanged tallies and established diplomatic relations, pacified the many Yue peoples so they would not become a threat on the southern frontier, where Nanyue bordered the Kingdom of Changsha.
translations.idiomatic: In the eleventh year of the Han dynasty, the emperor dispatched Lu Jia to formally install Tuo as the King of Nanyue. The two sides exchanged tallies and established diplomatic relations, with the goal of pacifying the various Yue peoples so they would not cause trouble along the southern border, where Nanyue adjoined the Kingdom of Changsha.

sentences.zh: 高后時，有司請禁南越關市鐵器。

translations.literal: During the time of Empress Dowager Gao, officials requested a ban on selling iron implements to Nanyue at the border markets.
translations.idiomatic: During the reign of Empress Dowager Gao, officials petitioned to ban the sale of iron implements to Nanyue through the border markets.

sentences.zh: 佗曰：「高帝立我，通使物，今高後聽讒臣，別異蠻夷，隔絕器物，此必長沙王計也，欲倚中國，擊滅南越而并王之，自為功也。」

translations.literal: Tuo said: "Emperor Gaozu installed me and opened trade and envoy exchanges. Now Empress Dowager Gao listens to slanderous ministers, treats us differently as barbarians, and cuts off our access to goods and implements. This must be a scheme of the King of Changsha, who wants to rely on the Central States to attack and destroy Nanyue, annex it, and claim the credit for himself."
translations.idiomatic: Tuo declared: "Emperor Gaozu installed me as king and opened trade and diplomatic relations. Now Empress Dowager Gao heeds the words of slanderous ministers, treats us as foreign barbarians, and cuts off our access to iron goods and implements. This is surely a plot by the King of Changsha, who wishes to lean on the might of the Central Plains to destroy Nanyue, annex our territory, and claim the glory for himself."

sentences.zh: 於是佗乃自尊號為南越武帝，發兵攻長沙邊邑，敗數縣而去焉。

translations.literal: Thereupon Tuo elevated his own title to Martial Emperor of Nanyue, raised troops to attack the border towns of Changsha, defeated several counties, and withdrew.
translations.idiomatic: At this, Tuo elevated his own title to Martial Emperor of Nanyue. He raised an army, attacked the border towns of Changsha, sacked several counties, and then withdrew.

sentences.zh: 高後遣將軍隆慮侯灶往擊之。

translations.literal: Empress Dowager Gao dispatched the general, the Marquis of Longlü, Zao, to go and attack him.
translations.idiomatic: Empress Dowager Gao dispatched General Zao, the Marquis of Longlü, to lead a campaign against him.

sentences.zh: 會暑溼，士卒大疫，兵不能踰嶺。

translations.literal: It happened that the summer heat and humidity caused a great plague among the soldiers, and the army could not cross the mountain passes.
translations.idiomatic: As it happened, the summer heat and humidity brought a devastating plague among the troops, and the army was unable to cross the mountain passes.

sentences.zh: 歲餘，高后崩，即罷兵。

translations.literal: After more than a year, Empress Dowager Gao died, and the campaign was immediately called off.
translations.idiomatic: Over a year later, Empress Dowager Gao died, and the campaign was promptly abandoned.

sentences.zh: 佗因此以兵威邊，財物賂遺閩越、西甌、駱，役屬焉，東西萬餘里。

translations.literal: Tuo then used military force to intimidate the border regions, and bribed Minyue, the Western Ou, and the Luo with wealth and goods, bringing them under his subordination. His territory stretched over ten thousand li from east to west.
translations.idiomatic: Tuo then used military might to cow the borderlands. He lavished gifts of wealth and goods upon the Minyue, the Western Ou, and the Luo, bringing them all under his dominion. His territory stretched more than ten thousand li from east to west.

sentences.zh: 乃乘黃屋左纛，稱制，與中國侔。

translations.literal: He then rode in a carriage with a yellow canopy and left-side pennant, issued imperial edicts, and rivaled the Central States in status.
translations.idiomatic: He rode in a carriage with the yellow canopy and left-side pennant reserved for an emperor, issued imperial edicts, and styled himself as the equal of the Han court.

sentences.zh: 及孝文帝元年，初鎮撫天下，使告諸侯四夷從代來即位意，喻盛德焉。

translations.literal: When Emperor Xiaowen ascended to the throne in his first year, he first calmed and pacified the realm. He sent messengers to inform the feudal lords and the barbarians of the four quarters that he had come from Dai to take the throne, proclaiming his great virtue.
translations.idiomatic: In the first year of Emperor Wen's reign, he set about bringing stability to the realm. He dispatched envoys to inform the feudal lords and the peoples of the four quarters that he had come from the Kingdom of Dai to take the throne, proclaiming his benevolent virtue.

sentences.zh: 乃為佗親冢在真定，置守邑，歲時奉祀。

translations.literal: Because Tuo's ancestral tombs were in Zhending, he appointed guardians for the settlement and had sacrificial rites performed at the proper seasons.
translations.idiomatic: Since Tuo's family tombs lay in Zhending, the emperor appointed custodians for the burial grounds and ordered that sacrificial rites be performed there at the proper seasons.

sentences.zh: 召其從昆弟，尊官厚賜寵之。

translations.literal: He summoned Tuo's cousins and relatives, bestowed high offices and generous gifts upon them, and showed them favor.
translations.idiomatic: He summoned Tuo's cousins and kinsmen, granted them high offices and generous gifts, and treated them with great favor.

sentences.zh: 詔丞相陳平等舉可使南越者，平言好畤陸賈，先帝時習使南越。

translations.literal: He ordered Chancellor Chen Ping and others to recommend someone suitable to send as envoy to Nanyue. Ping recommended Lu Jia of Haozhi, who had served as envoy to Nanyue during the previous emperor's reign.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor ordered Chancellor Chen Ping and others to recommend a suitable envoy to Nanyue. Chen Ping put forward Lu Jia of Haozhi, who had previously served as envoy to Nanyue under the former emperor.

sentences.zh: 乃召賈以為太中大夫，往使。

translations.literal: Lu Jia was then summoned and appointed Grand Palace Grandee, and sent as envoy.
translations.idiomatic: Lu Jia was accordingly summoned, appointed to the rank of Grand Palace Grandee, and dispatched as envoy.

sentences.zh: 因讓佗自立為帝，曾無一介之使報者。

translations.literal: He reproached Tuo for having proclaimed himself emperor, without having sent even a single envoy to report it.
translations.idiomatic: He also reproached Tuo for having proclaimed himself emperor without ever sending so much as a single envoy to inform the Han court.

sentences.zh: 陸賈至南越，王甚恐，為書謝，稱曰：「蠻夷大長老夫臣佗，前日高后隔異南越，竊疑長沙王讒臣，又遙聞高后盡誅佗宗族，掘燒先人冢，以故自棄，犯長沙邊境。

translations.literal: When Lu Jia arrived in Nanyue, the king was greatly alarmed. He composed a letter of apology, which read: "Your barbarian subject, the Great Elder Tuo: previously, when Empress Dowager Gao cut off Nanyue, I secretly suspected it was the King of Changsha slandering me. Then I heard from afar that Empress Dowager Gao had executed all of Tuo's kinsmen and dug up and burned the ancestral tombs. For this reason I abandoned restraint and attacked the border of Changsha."
translations.idiomatic: When Lu Jia reached Nanyue, the king was deeply alarmed. He composed a letter of apology, which read: "Your humble barbarian subject, the Great Elder Tuo: when Empress Dowager Gao cut off relations with Nanyue, I privately suspected the King of Changsha of slandering me at court. Then I heard from afar that the Empress Dowager had executed all of my kinsmen and dug up and burned the ancestral tombs. In my despair, I cast aside all restraint and attacked the Changsha border."

sentences.zh: 且南方卑溼，蠻夷中閒，其東閩越千人眾號稱王，其西甌駱裸國亦稱王。

translations.literal: Moreover, the south is low-lying and damp, situated among the barbarians. To the east, the Minyue, with a population of merely a thousand, style themselves king. To the west, the Ou and Luo, peoples of naked lands, also style themselves kings.
translations.idiomatic: "Moreover, the south is low-lying and damp, surrounded by barbarian peoples on all sides. To the east, the Minyue, with a mere thousand followers, style themselves kings. To the west, the Ou and the Luo, peoples of the uncultivated lands, likewise call themselves kings."

sentences.zh: 老臣妄竊帝號，聊以自娛，豈敢以聞天王哉！」

translations.literal: This old subject rashly usurped the imperial title merely to amuse himself. How would I dare bring this to the attention of the Heavenly King!
translations.idiomatic: "This old subject rashly assumed the imperial title merely to amuse himself. How would I dare bring such a matter to the attention of Your Heavenly Majesty!"

sentences.zh: 乃頓首謝，願長為藩臣，奉貢職。

translations.literal: He then bowed his head to the ground in apology and expressed his wish to remain a vassal forever, fulfilling his tribute obligations.
translations.idiomatic: He then prostrated himself in apology and pledged to serve forever as a loyal vassal, faithfully fulfilling his tribute obligations.

sentences.zh: 於是乃下令國中曰：「吾聞兩雄不俱立，兩賢不并世。

translations.literal: Thereupon he issued an edict throughout his kingdom, saying: "I have heard that two heroes cannot both stand, and two sages cannot coexist in the same age."
translations.idiomatic: He then issued a decree throughout his kingdom: "I have heard it said that two heroes cannot both stand, nor can two sages share the same age."

sentences.zh: 皇帝，賢天子也。

translations.literal: The Emperor is a worthy Son of Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: "The Emperor is a worthy Son of Heaven."

sentences.zh: 自今以後，去帝制黃屋左纛。」

translations.literal: From this day forward, I shall abolish the imperial style and relinquish the yellow canopy and left-side pennant.
translations.idiomatic: "From this day forward, I shall abandon the imperial title and relinquish the yellow canopy and left-side pennant."

sentences.zh: 陸賈還報，孝文帝大說。

translations.literal: When Lu Jia returned and reported, Emperor Xiaowen was greatly pleased.
translations.idiomatic: When Lu Jia returned with this report, Emperor Wen was greatly pleased.

sentences.zh: 遂至孝景時，稱臣，使人朝請。

translations.literal: Continuing through the reign of Emperor Xiaojing, Tuo called himself a subject and sent envoys to present tribute at court.
translations.idiomatic: Throughout the reign of Emperor Jing, Tuo continued to style himself a subject of the Han and sent envoys to pay tribute at court.

sentences.zh: 然南越其居國竊如故號名，其使天子，稱王朝命如諸侯。

translations.literal: However, within his own kingdom, Nanyue still secretly used the old imperial titles. When sending envoys to the Son of Heaven, he styled himself king and received court orders like the other feudal lords.
translations.idiomatic: Within his own domain, however, Nanyue still secretly retained its former imperial titles and customs. When dealing with the Son of Heaven, Tuo styled himself merely a king and received court orders as the other feudal lords did.

sentences.zh: 至建元四年卒。

translations.literal: He died in the fourth year of the Jianyuan era.
translations.idiomatic: He died in the fourth year of the Jianyuan era.

sentences.zh: 佗孫胡為南越王。

translations.literal: Tuo's grandson Hu became the King of Nanyue.
translations.idiomatic: Tuo's grandson Hu succeeded him as King of Nanyue.

sentences.zh: 此時閩越王郢興兵擊南越邊邑，胡使人上書曰：「兩越俱為藩臣，毋得擅興兵相攻擊。

translations.literal: At this time, the King of Minyue, Ying, raised troops to attack the border towns of Nanyue. Hu sent someone to present a memorial, saying: "Both Yue kingdoms are vassal states and should not be allowed to raise troops on their own initiative to attack each other."
translations.idiomatic: At this time, King Ying of Minyue raised an army and attacked the border towns of Nanyue. Hu sent a memorial to the throne, stating: "Both Yue kingdoms serve as vassals of the empire. Neither should be permitted to raise troops and attack the other without authorization."

sentences.zh: 今閩越興兵侵臣，臣不敢興兵，唯天子詔之。」

translations.literal: Now Minyue has raised troops to invade my territory. I dare not raise troops on my own, and await only the Son of Heaven's decree.
translations.idiomatic: "Now Minyue has raised an army to invade my territory. I dare not take up arms on my own, and respectfully await the Son of Heaven's command."

sentences.zh: 於是天子多南越義，守職約，為興師，遣兩將軍往討閩越。

translations.literal: Thereupon the Son of Heaven greatly commended Nanyue for its righteousness in keeping to its duty. He raised an army and dispatched two generals to go and punish Minyue.
translations.idiomatic: The Son of Heaven commended Nanyue's righteous adherence to its obligations. He mobilized an army and dispatched two generals to march against Minyue.

sentences.zh: 兵未踰嶺，閩越王弟餘善殺郢以降，於是罷兵。

translations.literal: Before the troops had crossed the mountain passes, the King of Minyue's younger brother Yu Shan killed Ying and surrendered, and the army was disbanded.
translations.idiomatic: Before the Han forces had even crossed the mountain passes, King Ying's younger brother Yu Shan killed him and surrendered. The campaign was thereupon called off.

sentences.zh: 天子使莊助往諭意南越王，胡頓首曰：「天子乃為臣興兵討閩越，死無以報德！」

translations.literal: The Son of Heaven sent Zhuang Zhu to go and convey his intentions to the King of Nanyue. Hu bowed his head to the ground and said: "The Son of Heaven raised an army on my behalf to punish Minyue. Even in death I could not repay this kindness!"
translations.idiomatic: The Son of Heaven sent Zhuang Zhu to convey his intentions to the King of Nanyue. Hu prostrated himself and declared: "The Son of Heaven raised an army on my behalf to punish Minyue. Even in death, I could never repay such a kindness!"

sentences.zh: 遣太子嬰齊入宿衛。

translations.literal: He sent his crown prince Yingqi to serve in the palace guard at the capital.
translations.idiomatic: He dispatched his crown prince, Yingqi, to serve in the palace guard at the Han capital.

sentences.zh: 謂助曰：「國新被寇，使者行矣。

translations.literal: He said to Zhu: "The kingdom has just suffered an invasion. The envoy may depart now.
translations.idiomatic: He told Zhuang Zhu: "Our kingdom has only just been invaded. You may take your leave now.

sentences.zh: 胡方日夜裝入見天子。」

translations.literal: Hu is preparing day and night to go and present himself before the Son of Heaven."
translations.idiomatic: I am making preparations day and night to travel to court and present myself before the Son of Heaven."

sentences.zh: 助去後，其大臣諫胡曰：「漢興兵誅郢，亦行以驚動南越。

translations.literal: After Zhu left, the senior ministers remonstrated with Hu, saying: "The Han raised an army to punish Ying, but also did so to intimidate Nanyue.
translations.idiomatic: After Zhuang Zhu departed, the senior ministers remonstrated with Hu: "The Han raised an army to punish Ying, but their purpose was also to intimidate Nanyue.

sentences.zh: 且先王昔言，事天子期無失禮，要之不可以說好語入見。

translations.literal: Moreover, the former king once said that in serving the Son of Heaven, one should aim not to fail in propriety, but above all must not be lured by sweet words into going to court in person.
translations.idiomatic: "Moreover, the former king once warned that in serving the Son of Heaven, the key is never to fail in courtesy—but under no circumstances should one be sweet-talked into appearing at court in person.

sentences.zh: 入見則不得復歸，亡國之勢也。」

translations.literal: If you go to court, you will never be allowed to return. This would mean the end of our kingdom."
translations.idiomatic: Once you go to court, you will never be permitted to return. That would mean the ruin of our kingdom."

sentences.zh: 於是胡稱病，竟不入見。

translations.literal: Thereupon Hu claimed illness and in the end never went to court.
translations.idiomatic: At this, Hu pleaded illness and ultimately never went to court.

sentences.zh: 後十餘歲，胡實病甚，太子嬰齊請歸。

translations.literal: More than ten years later, Hu truly fell seriously ill, and Crown Prince Yingqi requested permission to return home.
translations.idiomatic: More than ten years later, Hu genuinely fell gravely ill. Crown Prince Yingqi asked permission to return home.

sentences.zh: 胡薨，謚為文王。

translations.literal: Hu died and was posthumously titled King Wen.
translations.idiomatic: When Hu died, he was given the posthumous title of King Wen, the Cultured King.

sentences.zh: 嬰齊代立，即藏其先武帝璽。

translations.literal: Yingqi succeeded to the throne and immediately concealed the imperial seal of the former Martial Emperor.
translations.idiomatic: When Yingqi took the throne, he immediately hid away the imperial seal that had belonged to the earlier Martial Emperor.

sentences.zh: 嬰齊其入宿衛在長安時，取邯鄲樛氏女，生子興。

translations.literal: When Yingqi had served in the palace guard in Chang'an, he took a woman of the Jiu clan of Handan and fathered a son named Xing.
translations.idiomatic: During his time serving in the palace guard at Chang'an, Yingqi had taken a woman of the Jiu clan from Handan as his consort and fathered a son named Xing.

sentences.zh: 及即位，上書請立樛氏女為后，興為嗣。

translations.literal: Upon ascending the throne, he submitted a memorial requesting that the woman of the Jiu clan be made queen, and Xing be named heir.
translations.idiomatic: Upon his accession, he petitioned the Han court to recognize the Jiu woman as his queen and Xing as his heir.

sentences.zh: 漢數使使者風諭嬰齊，嬰齊尚樂擅殺生自恣，懼入見要用漢法，比內諸侯，固稱病，遂不入見。

translations.literal: The Han repeatedly sent envoys to hint at and urge Yingqi to come to court. However, Yingqi still enjoyed the power to execute at will and indulge himself freely. He feared that if he went to court, he would be required to follow Han law and be treated as an inner feudal lord. He stubbornly pleaded illness and ultimately never went to court.
translations.idiomatic: The Han court repeatedly sent envoys to urge Yingqi to present himself at court. But Yingqi relished the power to pass life-and-death judgments at will and to indulge himself as he pleased. He feared that appearing at court would mean submitting to Han law and being treated as an inner feudal lord. He stubbornly pleaded illness and never went.

sentences.zh: 遣子次公入宿衛。

translations.literal: He sent his son Cigong to serve in the palace guard.
translations.idiomatic: Instead, he sent his son Cigong to serve in the palace guard at the capital.

sentences.zh: 嬰齊薨，謚為明王。

translations.literal: When Yingqi died, he was posthumously titled King Ming.
translations.idiomatic: When Yingqi died, he was given the posthumous title of King Ming, the Brilliant King.

sentences.zh: 太子興代立，其母為太后。

translations.literal: Crown Prince Xing succeeded to the throne, and his mother became the Queen Dowager.
translations.idiomatic: Crown Prince Xing succeeded to the throne, and his mother was elevated to the rank of Queen Dowager.

sentences.zh: 太后自未為嬰齊姬時，嘗與霸陵人安國少季通。

translations.literal: Before the Queen Dowager had become Yingqi's consort, she had once had an affair with An Guoshaoji, a man of Baling.
translations.idiomatic: Even before the Queen Dowager had become Yingqi's consort, she had carried on an affair with a man named An Guoshaoji from Baling.

sentences.zh: 及嬰齊薨後，元鼎四年，漢使安國少季往諭王、王太后以入朝，比內諸侯；

translations.literal: After Yingqi died, in the fourth year of the Yuanding era, the Han sent An Guoshaoji to persuade the king and the Queen Dowager to come to court and accept the status of inner feudal lords.
translations.idiomatic: After Yingqi's death, in the fourth year of the Yuanding era, the Han court dispatched An Guoshaoji to persuade the king and the Queen Dowager to present themselves at court and accept the status of inner feudal lords.

sentences.zh: 令辯士諫大夫終軍等宣其辭，勇士魏臣等輔其缺，衛尉路博德將兵屯桂陽，待使者。

translations.literal: The eloquent Remonstrance Grandee Zhong Jun and others were ordered to deliver the persuasive arguments, the brave warrior Wei Chen and others to supplement their shortcomings, and the Commandant of Guards Lu Bode to station troops at Guiyang and await the envoys.
translations.idiomatic: The court also ordered the eloquent Remonstrance Grandee Zhong Jun and others to present the arguments, the brave warrior Wei Chen and others to provide military backing, and the Commandant of Guards Lu Bode to station troops at Guiyang in readiness to support the envoys.

sentences.zh: 王年少，太後中國人也，嘗與安國少季通，其使復私焉。

translations.literal: The king was young. The Queen Dowager was originally from the Central States and had previously had an affair with An Guoshaoji. When he came as envoy, she resumed their private relationship.
translations.idiomatic: The king was young, and the Queen Dowager, who was originally from the Central Plains, had previously been An Guoshaoji's lover. When he arrived as envoy, the two resumed their affair.

sentences.zh: 國人頗知之，多不附太后。

translations.literal: The people of the kingdom became quite aware of this and many did not support the Queen Dowager.
translations.idiomatic: The people of the kingdom were well aware of the affair, and many withheld their support from the Queen Dowager.

sentences.zh: 太后恐亂起，亦欲倚漢威，數勸王及群臣求內屬。

translations.literal: The Queen Dowager feared that disorder would break out. She also wished to rely on the power of the Han, and repeatedly urged the king and the ministers to seek the status of inner subjects.
translations.idiomatic: The Queen Dowager, fearing that unrest might break out, sought to rely on the might of the Han dynasty. She repeatedly urged the king and his ministers to petition for the status of inner subjects of the Han.

sentences.zh: 即因使者上書，請比內諸侯，三歲一朝，除邊關。

translations.literal: Through the envoys, a memorial was submitted requesting the status of inner feudal lords, to pay court once every three years, and to abolish the border passes.
translations.idiomatic: Through the envoys, a memorial was submitted requesting that Nanyue be treated as an inner feudal state, pay court once every three years, and have the border passes abolished.

sentences.zh: 於是天子許之，賜其丞相呂嘉銀印，及內史、中尉、太傅印，餘得自置。

translations.literal: The Son of Heaven approved the request and bestowed silver seals upon the Chancellor Lu Jia, as well as seals for the Inner Historian, the Colonel of the Guard, and the Grand Tutor. The remaining officials could be appointed by Nanyue itself.
translations.idiomatic: The Son of Heaven approved the request. He bestowed silver seals upon the chancellor, Lu Jia, as well as seals for the positions of Inner Historian, Colonel of the Guard, and Grand Tutor. The remaining officials could be appointed by Nanyue at its own discretion.

sentences.zh: 除其故黥劓刑，用漢法，比內諸侯。

translations.literal: The old punishments of branding and nose-cutting were abolished, Han law was adopted, and Nanyue was treated on par with the inner feudal lords.
translations.idiomatic: The old punishments of tattooing and nose-cutting were abolished, Han law was adopted, and Nanyue was treated on the same footing as the inner feudal lords.

sentences.zh: 使者皆留填撫之。

translations.literal: The envoys all remained to garrison and pacify the kingdom.
translations.idiomatic: All the envoys remained in Nanyue to help maintain order and stability.

sentences.zh: 王、王太后飭治行裝重齎，為入朝具。

translations.literal: The king and the Queen Dowager prepared their travel equipment and lavish gifts, making ready for the journey to court.
translations.idiomatic: The king and the Queen Dowager began outfitting their traveling retinue and assembling lavish gifts in preparation for the journey to court.

sentences.zh: 其相呂嘉年長矣，相三王，宗族官仕為長吏者七十餘人，男盡尚王女，女盡嫁王子兄弟宗室，及蒼梧秦王有連。

translations.literal: The chancellor, Lu Jia, was advanced in years and had served as chancellor to three kings. Over seventy members of his clan held senior offices. All his sons had married daughters of the royal house, and all his daughters had married princes, royal brothers, or members of the royal clan. He also had marital ties to Zhao Guang, the King of Cangwu.
translations.idiomatic: The chancellor, Lu Jia, was advanced in years. He had served as chancellor under three successive kings, and more than seventy members of his clan held senior offices. Every one of his sons had married a princess, and every one of his daughters had married a prince or a member of the royal clan. He was also linked by marriage to Zhao Guang, the King of Cangwu.

sentences.zh: 其居國中甚重，越人信之，多為耳目者，得眾心愈於王。

translations.literal: His standing within the kingdom was very high. The Yue people trusted him and many served as his informants. He commanded the hearts of the populace even more than the king did.
translations.idiomatic: His influence within the kingdom was immense. The Yue people placed their trust in him, and many served as his eyes and ears. He commanded the loyalty of the populace even more than the king himself.

sentences.zh: 王之上書，數諫止王，王弗聽。

translations.literal: When the king submitted his memorial, Lu Jia repeatedly remonstrated to stop the king, but the king would not listen.
translations.idiomatic: When the king submitted his memorial to the Han court, Lu Jia remonstrated repeatedly against it, but the king refused to listen.

sentences.zh: 有畔心，數稱病不見漢使者。

translations.literal: He harbored intentions of rebellion, and repeatedly claimed illness to avoid meeting the Han envoys.
translations.idiomatic: Lu Jia harbored thoughts of rebellion. He repeatedly pleaded illness and refused to meet with the Han envoys.

sentences.zh: 使者皆注意嘉，勢未能誅。

translations.literal: The envoys all kept a close watch on Lu Jia, but they did not yet have the power to execute him.
translations.idiomatic: The envoys all had their eyes on Lu Jia, but they lacked the means to eliminate him.

sentences.zh: 王、王太后亦恐嘉等先事發，乃置酒，介漢使者權，謀誅嘉等。

translations.literal: The king and the Queen Dowager also feared that Lu Jia and the others would strike first. They arranged a banquet, relying on the authority of the Han envoys, and plotted to kill Lu Jia and his associates.
translations.idiomatic: The king and the Queen Dowager, fearing that Lu Jia might strike first, arranged a banquet. They planned to use the presence and authority of the Han envoys to assassinate Lu Jia and his faction.

sentences.zh: 使者皆東鄉，太后南鄉，王北鄉，相嘉、大臣皆西鄉，侍坐飲。

translations.literal: The envoys all faced east, the Queen Dowager faced south, the king faced north, and Chancellor Lu Jia and the senior ministers all faced west, attending the banquet seated.
translations.idiomatic: At the banquet, the envoys were seated facing east, the Queen Dowager facing south, the king facing north, and Chancellor Lu Jia and the senior ministers all facing west.

sentences.zh: 嘉弟為將，將卒居宮外。

translations.literal: Lu Jia's younger brother served as a general and was stationed with his troops outside the palace.
translations.idiomatic: Lu Jia's younger brother, who was a general, had his soldiers stationed just outside the palace.

sentences.zh: 酒行，太后謂嘉曰：「南越內屬，國之利也，而相君苦不便者，何也？」

translations.literal: As the wine was served, the Queen Dowager said to Lu Jia: "For Nanyue to submit as an inner subject would be to the kingdom's benefit. Yet you, Lord Chancellor, insist it is inconvenient. Why is that?"
translations.idiomatic: As the wine flowed, the Queen Dowager turned to Lu Jia and said: "For Nanyue to submit as an inner subject would serve the kingdom's best interests. Yet you, Lord Chancellor, keep objecting that it is inconvenient. Why?"

sentences.zh: 以激怒使者。

translations.literal: This was intended to provoke the envoys into action.
translations.idiomatic: She said this to provoke the envoys into acting against Lu Jia.

sentences.zh: 使者狐疑相杖，遂莫敢發。

translations.literal: The envoys hesitated and looked at one another for support, and in the end none of them dared to act.
translations.idiomatic: But the envoys hesitated, glancing at one another uncertainly. In the end, none of them dared to make a move.

sentences.zh: 嘉見耳目非是，即起而出。

translations.literal: When Lu Jia saw that the situation was not right, he immediately rose and left.
translations.idiomatic: When Lu Jia sensed that something was amiss, he rose at once and left the banquet.

sentences.zh: 太后怒，欲鏦嘉以矛，王止太後。

translations.literal: The Queen Dowager was furious and wanted to stab Lu Jia with a spear, but the king restrained her.
translations.idiomatic: The Queen Dowager flew into a rage and wanted to strike Lu Jia down with a spear, but the king held her back.

sentences.zh: 嘉遂出，分其弟兵就舍，稱病，不肯見王及使者。

translations.literal: Lu Jia then left and took a portion of his brother's troops to his own quarters. He claimed illness and refused to see the king or the envoys.
translations.idiomatic: Lu Jia withdrew, deployed a portion of his brother's soldiers to guard his own quarters, and claimed illness. He refused to see either the king or the envoys.

sentences.zh: 乃陰與大臣作亂。

translations.literal: He then secretly plotted rebellion with the senior ministers.
translations.idiomatic: He then began secretly plotting a revolt with the other senior ministers.

sentences.zh: 王素無意誅嘉，嘉知之，以故數月不發。

translations.literal: The king had never actually intended to execute Lu Jia. Lu Jia knew this, and for this reason he did not act for several months.
translations.idiomatic: The king had never truly intended to execute Lu Jia, and Lu Jia knew it. For this reason, he bided his time for several months without making a move.

sentences.zh: 太后有淫行，國人不附，欲獨誅嘉等，力又不能。

translations.literal: The Queen Dowager had engaged in licentious conduct, and the people did not support her. She wished to execute Lu Jia and his associates on her own, but lacked the power to do so.
translations.idiomatic: The Queen Dowager's scandalous conduct had cost her the people's support. She wished to eliminate Lu Jia and his faction single-handedly, but she lacked the power to do so.

sentences.zh: 天子聞嘉不聽王，王、王太后弱孤不能制，使者怯無決。

translations.literal: The Son of Heaven heard that Lu Jia refused to obey the king, that the king and the Queen Dowager were weak and isolated and could not control him, and that the envoys were timid and irresolute.
translations.idiomatic: When the Son of Heaven learned that Lu Jia defied the king, that the king and the Queen Dowager were too weak and isolated to control him, and that the envoys were timid and indecisive, he took action.

sentences.zh: 又以為王、王太后已附漢，獨呂嘉為亂，不足以興兵，欲使莊參以二千人往使。

translations.literal: He also believed that since the king and Queen Dowager had already submitted to Han, it was Lu Jia alone who was causing trouble. This was not sufficient grounds to raise a full army. He wished to send Zhuang Can with two thousand men as an envoy.
translations.idiomatic: He also reckoned that since the king and the Queen Dowager had already aligned with the Han, it was Lu Jia alone who was causing trouble. This did not warrant a full military campaign. He planned to send Zhuang Can with two thousand men on a diplomatic mission.

sentences.zh: 參曰：「以好往，數人足矣；

translations.literal: Zhuang Can said: "If the mission is a friendly one, a few men will suffice.
translations.idiomatic: Zhuang Can replied: "If the purpose is diplomacy, a handful of men will do.

sentences.zh: 以武往，二千人無足以為也。」

translations.literal: If the mission is a military one, two thousand men are far from sufficient."
translations.idiomatic: If the purpose is war, two thousand men are nowhere near enough."

sentences.zh: 辭不可，天子罷參也。

translations.literal: He firmly declined, and the Son of Heaven dismissed Zhuang Can from the assignment.
translations.idiomatic: He firmly declined the assignment, and the Son of Heaven relieved him of it.

sentences.zh: 郟壯士故濟北相韓千秋奮曰：「以區區之越，又有王、太后應，獨相呂嘉為害，願得勇士二百人，必斬嘉以報。」

translations.literal: The bold warrior Han Qianqiu from Jia, the former Chancellor of Jibei, stepped forward and declared: "Against such a small state as Yue, and with the king and Queen Dowager on our side, it is only Chancellor Lu Jia who is causing trouble. Give me two hundred brave men, and I will surely behead Lu Jia and bring back his head."
translations.idiomatic: Han Qianqiu, a bold warrior from Jia and former Chancellor of Jibei, stepped forward and declared: "Yue is a trifling state, and the king and the Queen Dowager are on our side. Only Chancellor Lu Jia stands in the way. Give me two hundred brave men, and I will take Lu Jia's head."

sentences.zh: 於是天子遣千秋與王太后弟樛樂將二千人往，入越境。

translations.literal: The Son of Heaven then dispatched Han Qianqiu, together with the Queen Dowager's younger brother Jiu Le, to lead two thousand men into Yue territory.
translations.idiomatic: The Son of Heaven then dispatched Han Qianqiu along with the Queen Dowager's younger brother, Jiu Le, at the head of two thousand soldiers to enter Yue territory.

sentences.zh: 呂嘉等乃遂反，下令國中曰：「王年少。

translations.literal: Lu Jia and his faction then launched their rebellion and issued a proclamation throughout the kingdom: "The king is young.
translations.idiomatic: Lu Jia and his faction then launched their rebellion in earnest, issuing a proclamation throughout the kingdom: "The king is young.

sentences.zh: 太後，中國人也，又與使者亂，專欲內屬，盡持先王寶器入獻天子以自媚，多從人，行至長安，虜賣以為僮仆。

translations.literal: The Queen Dowager is a woman from the Central States who has had illicit relations with the envoy. She is intent on submitting to Han as an inner subject, taking all the treasures of the former kings to present to the Son of Heaven in order to curry favor. She plans to bring many of our people with her to Chang'an, where they will be captured and sold as slaves and servants.
translations.idiomatic: The Queen Dowager is a woman from the Central Plains who has carried on a sordid affair with the envoy. She is bent on submitting our kingdom to the Han, carrying off all the treasures of the former kings to present to the Son of Heaven and ingratiate herself. She plans to take many of our people along to Chang'an, where they will be seized and sold into servitude.

sentences.zh: 取自脫一時之利，無顧趙氏社稷，為萬世慮計之意。」

translations.literal: She seeks only to save herself for a moment's advantage, with no thought for the Zhao ancestral altars or for plans that would last ten thousand generations."
translations.idiomatic: She seeks only to save herself for a fleeting advantage, with no thought for the Zhao ancestral altars or for the kingdom's future across ten thousand generations."

sentences.zh: 乃與其弟將卒攻殺王、太后及漢使者。

translations.literal: Lu Jia and his brother then led their soldiers in an attack, killing the king, the Queen Dowager, and the Han envoys.
translations.idiomatic: Lu Jia and his brother then led their soldiers in an assault on the palace, killing the king, the Queen Dowager, and the Han envoys.

sentences.zh: 遣人告蒼梧秦王及其諸郡縣，立明王長男越妻子術陽侯建德為王。

translations.literal: He sent men to inform the Qin King of Cangwu and all the commanderies and counties, and installed King Ming's eldest son Jiande, the Marquis of Shuyang, who was born of a Yue wife, as the new king.
translations.idiomatic: He sent word to the Qin King of Cangwu and to all the commanderies and counties, and installed as the new king Jiande, the Marquis of Shuyang, who was King Ming's eldest son by a Yue wife.

sentences.zh: 而韓千秋兵入，破數小邑。

translations.literal: Meanwhile, Han Qianqiu's forces entered Yue and captured several small towns.
translations.idiomatic: Meanwhile, Han Qianqiu's forces crossed into Yue and overran several small towns.

sentences.zh: 其後越直開道給食，未至番禺四十里，越以兵擊千秋等，遂滅之。

translations.literal: Afterwards, the Yue deliberately opened the roads and provided provisions. When they were within forty li of Panyu, the Yue attacked Han Qianqiu and his men with their forces, and annihilated them all.
translations.idiomatic: The Yue then deliberately cleared the roads and provided provisions to lure them deeper. When the Han force was within forty li of Panyu, the Yue attacked and annihilated Han Qianqiu and all his men.

sentences.zh: 使人函封漢使者節置塞上，好為謾辭謝罪，發兵守要害處。

translations.literal: They then had the credentials of the Han envoys sealed in a box and placed at the frontier pass. They composed deceitful words of apology and deployed troops to guard the strategic positions.
translations.idiomatic: They sealed the credentials of the Han envoys in a case and left them at the frontier pass. They composed a disingenuous letter of apology and deployed troops to guard the strategic passes.

sentences.zh: 於是天子曰：「韓千秋雖無成功，亦軍鋒之冠。」

translations.literal: The Son of Heaven said: "Although Han Qianqiu did not succeed, he was still the foremost in martial courage."
translations.idiomatic: The Son of Heaven declared: "Although Han Qianqiu did not succeed, he was the vanguard of our military valor."

sentences.zh: 封其子延年為成安侯。

translations.literal: He enfeoffed Han Qianqiu's son Yannian as the Marquis of Cheng'an.
translations.idiomatic: He enfeoffed Han Qianqiu's son Yannian as the Marquis of Cheng'an.

sentences.zh: 樛樂，其姊為王太后，首願屬漢，封其子廣德為龍亢侯。

translations.literal: As for Jiu Le, whose elder sister was the Queen Dowager, she had been the first to express willingness to submit to Han. Her son Guangde was enfeoffed as the Marquis of Longkang.
translations.idiomatic: As for Jiu Le, whose elder sister had been the Queen Dowager and the first to advocate submission to the Han—his son Guangde was enfeoffed as the Marquis of Longkang.

sentences.zh: 乃下赦曰：「天子微，諸侯力政，譏臣不討賊。

translations.literal: The emperor then issued an edict saying: "When the Son of Heaven is weak, the feudal lords rule by force, and ministers are reproached for not punishing rebels.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor then issued an edict: "When the Son of Heaven's authority wanes, the feudal lords seize power by force, and ministers are condemned for failing to punish rebels.

sentences.zh: 今呂嘉、建德等反，自立晏如，令罪人及江淮以南樓船十萬師往討之。」

translations.literal: Now Lu Jia, Jiande, and the others have rebelled and established themselves as though nothing were amiss. I hereby order convicts and one hundred thousand tower-ship soldiers from south of the Yangzi and Huai rivers to go and punish them."
translations.idiomatic: Now Lu Jia, Jiande, and the rest have risen in rebellion and sit at ease as though nothing were amiss. I hereby command that convicts and a hundred thousand tower-ship soldiers from south of the Yangzi and Huai be dispatched to crush them."

sentences.zh: 元鼎五年秋，衛尉路博德為伏波將軍，出桂陽，下匯水；

translations.literal: In the autumn of the fifth year of the Yuanding era, the Commandant of Guards Lu Bode was appointed General Who Calms the Waves. He set out from Guiyang and descended the Hui River.
translations.idiomatic: In the autumn of the fifth year of the Yuanding era, the Commandant of Guards Lu Bode was appointed General Who Calms the Waves. He marched out from Guiyang and descended the Hui River.

sentences.zh: 主爵都尉楊仆為樓船將軍，出豫章，下橫浦；

translations.literal: The Commandant of Titles Yang Pu was appointed General of the Tower Ships. He set out from Yuzhang and descended through the Hengpu Pass.
translations.idiomatic: Yang Pu, the Commandant of Titles, was appointed General of the Tower Ships. He marched out from Yuzhang and descended through the Hengpu Pass.

sentences.zh: 故歸義越侯二人為戈船、下厲將軍，出零陵，或下離水，或柢蒼梧；

translations.literal: Two former Yue marquises who had submitted to the Han were appointed the General of the War-Boat and the General of the Lower Li. They set out from Lingling, one descending the Li River and the other heading for Cangwu.
translations.idiomatic: Two former Yue marquises who had submitted to the Han were appointed General of the War-Boats and General of the Lower Li. They set out from Lingling—one descending the Li River, the other advancing toward Cangwu.

sentences.zh: 使馳義侯因巴蜀罪人，發夜郎兵，下牂柯江：咸會番禺。

translations.literal: The Marquis Who Gallops for Righteousness was ordered to use convicts from Ba and Shu, mobilize the troops of Yelang, and descend the Zangke River. All were to converge on Panyu.
translations.idiomatic: The Marquis Who Gallops for Righteousness was ordered to recruit convicts from Ba and Shu, mobilize the soldiers of Yelang, and descend the Zangke River. All forces were to converge on Panyu.

sentences.zh: 元鼎六年冬，樓船將軍將精卒先陷尋陜，破石門，得越船粟，因推而前，挫越鋒，以數萬人待伏波。

translations.literal: In the winter of the sixth year of the Yuanding era, the General of the Tower Ships led his elite troops to first take Xunxia, broke through Shimen, captured Yue boats and grain, and pressed forward, blunting the Yue vanguard. He then waited with tens of thousands of men for the General Who Calms the Waves.
translations.idiomatic: In the winter of the sixth year of the Yuanding era, the General of the Tower Ships led his elite troops in capturing Xunxia first, then broke through the Stone Gate, seized Yue boats and grain stores, and pressed forward, blunting the Yue vanguard. He halted with tens of thousands of troops to await the General Who Calms the Waves.

sentences.zh: 伏波將軍將罪人，道遠，會期後，與樓船會乃有千餘人，遂俱進。

translations.literal: The General Who Calms the Waves led convict soldiers. The road was long, he missed the rendezvous deadline, and when he joined up with the Tower Ships general, he had only a little more than a thousand men. They then advanced together.
translations.idiomatic: The General Who Calms the Waves, leading his convict soldiers, had a long march and arrived after the appointed time. When he linked up with the Tower Ships general, he had only a little over a thousand men. Nevertheless, the two forces advanced together.

sentences.zh: 樓船居前，至番禺。

translations.literal: The Tower Ships general took the lead and reached Panyu.
translations.idiomatic: The Tower Ships general took the vanguard and arrived at Panyu.

sentences.zh: 建德、嘉皆城守。

translations.literal: Jiande and Lu Jia both defended the city.
translations.idiomatic: Jiande and Lu Jia had fortified the city and held their positions.

sentences.zh: 樓船自擇便處，居東南面；

translations.literal: The Tower Ships general chose a favorable position and took the southeast side of the city.
translations.idiomatic: The Tower Ships general chose a favorable position on the southeast side of the city.

sentences.zh: 伏波居西北面。

translations.literal: The General Who Calms the Waves took the northwest side.
translations.idiomatic: The General Who Calms the Waves took up position on the northwest side.

sentences.zh: 會暮，樓船攻敗越人，縱火燒城。

translations.literal: At dusk, the Tower Ships general attacked and defeated the Yue defenders, and set fire to the city.
translations.idiomatic: As dusk fell, the Tower Ships general launched his attack and defeated the Yue defenders, then set fire to the city.

sentences.zh: 越素聞伏波名，日暮，不知其兵多少。

translations.literal: The Yue had long heard of the reputation of the General Who Calms the Waves. Now that darkness had fallen, they did not know the size of his forces.
translations.idiomatic: The Yue had long heard of the fearsome reputation of the General Who Calms the Waves. In the darkness, they had no way of knowing the size of his forces.

sentences.zh: 伏波乃為營，遣使者招降者，賜印，復縱令相招。
樓船力攻燒敵，反驅而入伏波營中。
犁旦，城中皆降伏波。
呂嘉、建德已夜與其屬數百人亡入海，以船西去。
伏波又因問所得降者貴人，以知呂嘉所之，遣人追之。
以其故校尉司馬蘇弘得建德，封為海常侯；
越郎都稽得嘉，封為臨蔡侯。
蒼梧王趙光者，越王同姓，聞漢兵至，及越揭陽令定自定屬漢；
越桂林監居翁諭甌駱屬漢：皆得為侯。
戈船、下厲將軍兵及馳義侯所發夜郎兵未下，南越已平矣。
遂為九郡。
伏波將軍益封。
樓船將軍兵以陷堅為將梁侯。
自尉佗初王後，五世九十三歲而國亡焉。
太史公曰：尉佗之王，本由任囂。
遭漢初定，列為諸侯。
隆慮離溼疫，佗得以益驕。
甌駱相攻，南越動搖。
漢兵臨境，嬰齊入朝。
其後亡國，徵自樛女；
呂嘉小忠，令佗無後。
樓船從欲，怠傲失惑；
伏波困窮，智慮愈殖，因禍為福。
成敗之轉，譬若糾墨。
